comments.author: @GlobalTales68
comments.text: I really enjoyed this perspective on life in Uzbekistan. Even though it’s featured as the cheapest country in the world, the warmth of the people and the vibrant food culture make it look incredibly rich. This travel documentary does a fantastic job showing that true value isn't just about money!

comments.author: @xavytex
comments.text:
Just came back from Uzbekistan. It’s only half price compared to Canada. It’s not as cheap as youtubers say it is.
Vegetables are very expensive ($4 a kilo of tomatoes, $7 a kilo of bell pepper, that’s the same price as Canada).
The only thing that I found was cheap was taxi. About $1.5 for a 15 minutes ride.
Don’t go there to save money. Go there if you have an interest for islamic design. People don’t speak English so don’t expect to have good conversations with the locals unless you speak Russian.
